_doc: Methodology pin. The single declaration of every input and parameter that affects analytical output. Every artifact (snapshot, briefing, metrics, analysis, draft) is stamped with `meta_version` so longitudinal consumers can tell which era it belongs to. Bump rules: patch=no output change; minor=forward-compatible (added story, added feed); major=invalidates longitudinal comparison (swapped cross-bucket similarity, swapped embedding model, removed buckets). Bump via `python baseline_pin.py --bump <level> --reason '...'`. CI enforces hash match via `python -m meta` on every PR.
pin_reason: revert embedding model to e5-large (the stronger model); fix latest_snapshot_date picking up _embedding_ids/_clusters sibling files; add EMBEDDING_MODEL override + scripts/smoke_pipeline.py

tokenizer._doc: Unicode-aware tokenizer via the `regex` library. \p{L} matches any Unicode letter so diacritics in any script (e.g. 'México', 'résumé', 'göteborg') are preserved as single tokens. Lower bound 4 chars stabilises minimum vocabulary size. Operates on the original signal_text in its source language; the multilingual stopwords union (NLTK + spaCy) filters function words across the languages we ingest. Used for bucket-exclusive vocabulary; cross-bucket similarity is LaBSE (see metrics).

clustering._doc: Primary clusterer is HDBSCAN as of meta_version 6.0.0 (Phase C.5). Density-adaptive; no eps to justify; exposes per-cluster stability scores via cluster_topics.last_stability_scores. Falls back to legacy DBSCAN (eps + min_samples below) when hdbscan isn't installed. Calibration: see archive/clustering_calibration_*.md. The k-distance plot used to justify the prior DBSCAN eps=0.35 is no longer authoritative; HDBSCAN derives the equivalent threshold per-density.

metrics._doc: Cross-bucket similarity. As of 7.0.0 the primary metric is LaBSE bucket-mean cosine on original signal_text (no translation pivot — LaBSE is multilingual by construction). Bucket-exclusive vocabulary uses the pinned tokeniser + multilingual stopwords union on the same originals; analyzer is instructed to flag language-confounded distinctive terms.

claude._doc: Dated snapshot ID (claude-<model>-<version>-YYYYMMDD) pins model weights as far as the API permits — the alias may roll forward as Anthropic ships updates. Anthropic deprecates snapshots after ~12 months; bump major when a snapshot is end-of-life and re-baseline. Drift between snapshot and what is actually served can be checked manually via `python -m canary.run`. v8.0.0 swap from sonnet to haiku is a deliberate test-pass swap on the simplification branch — see pin_reason.

perception._doc: v10 article embedding. Every article in the daily snapshot is encoded with `embedding_model`; the vectors feed core/cluster/cluster_daily.py (HDBSCAN over the full day). `signal_text_version` is a cache-invalidation knob — it composes into the article_id hash, so bumping it OR `embedding_model` forces a clean re-encode. Model: intfloat/multilingual-e5-large (1024-dim) — the strongest multilingual option; encoding the full ~6,500-article day takes ~25-30 min on the 2-core runner, which is why the ingest job has a 60-min budget. The `EMBEDDING_MODEL` env var overrides this at runtime (used by scripts/smoke_pipeline.py for fast local logic tests). (Block name kept as `perception` for the meta.PERCEPTION accessor; the v9 softmax-argmax matcher it was named for retired in v10.)
